pattern: A left-to-right dynamic program tries every length of the final block, replacing it with its running maximum, so each prefix's best sum builds on shorter prefixes.
hints: Think about the last block of the partition: it can be any length from 1 up to k.
If you know the best sum for every shorter prefix, the answer for position i tries each possible final block ending there.
Let dp[i] be the best sum for the first i elements; for j from 1..k track the running max of the last j elements and take dp[i-j] + maxInBlock*j.

edge_inputs.description: block size three lifts the small values
k of one forces no grouping benefit

stress.description: moderate array with random values and a random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    arr = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [arr, k]
stress.note: values bounded to keep the 32-bit guarantee comfortable
